safest_plan.entry_basis: zero_hub chart (2026-06-10): top of the green 'Whole DCA area' buy zone at $419; patient scale-in adds lower at $399 (0.618 fib) and $393, with deeper averaging into the $350 (PDB) and $330 demand blocks if reached

safest_plan.stop_basis: below the $330 demand block / PDB structure on the zero_hub chart — the deepest support before the low-probability 2004 HTF breaker; a clean break there negates the accumulation thesis

stance_by_source.summary: Accumulate spot patiently; near-term resistance sits around 430.
Stagger spot buys across the $393-419 zone (deeper to $350/$330) and take profit up at $475, $600 and $650.
Positioning table (COT, as of 2026-05-12) — context only, no trade levels; commercials cut shorts heavily.

conflicts: The newer 06-15 note caps near-term resistance at ~430, while the 06-10 chart's broader fair-value/resistance sits higher (~$462-475) — agree on direction but the near-term move is capped tighter than the longer-range targets imply.
Current price (~422.75) is sitting just above the top of the buy zone ($419), so this is not yet an at-entry setup — you are waiting for price to come into the zone, not buying here.
plain_english: Every source points the same way on corn: buy slowly into weakness, don't chase. The plan is to add in stages as price drops into roughly the $393-$419 area (and average lower toward $350-$330 if it keeps falling), aiming to sell back out into the $475-$650 range over time. The thesis only breaks if price falls cleanly below $330. Price right now is just above the buy zone and capped near $430 in the short term, so the action is to wait for it to dip into the zone rather than buy at the current level.
